<!--
Parked exhibits — removed from the rendered book to streamline it (2026-07-08), kept here so
any of them can be restored by copying the block back into its source chapter. This file is NOT
rendered: it is not listed in _quarto.yml and its underscore prefix excludes it from the project.
Each block notes its source chapter and the setup objects it relied on.
-->

# Parked exhibits (not rendered)

## From "Where do donors live?" — donor households by distance band (chart)

Relied on `by_band` from the chapter's setup chunk. The same numbers remain in the chapter's
table.

```{python}
#| label: fig-households-band
#| fig-cap: "Donor households by distance band (miles)"
fig = px.bar(by_band, x="distance_band", y="households", color_discrete_sequence=[BLUE])
fig.update_layout(
    showlegend=False,
    xaxis_title="Distance from Hubbard Hall (miles)",
    yaxis_title="Donor households",
)
fig.update_traces(hovertemplate="%{y:,.0f} households<extra></extra>")
style(fig).show()
```

## From "Where do donors live?" — average donation per household by band (chart)

Relied on `by_band`. The same numbers remain in the chapter's table (`mean_gift`).

```{python}
#| label: fig-avg-donation-band
#| fig-cap: "Average donation per donor household by distance band (miles)"
fig = px.bar(by_band, x="distance_band", y="mean_gift", color_discrete_sequence=[BLUE])
fig.update_layout(
    showlegend=False,
    xaxis_title="Distance from Hubbard Hall (miles)",
    yaxis_title="Average donation per household",
)
fig.update_yaxes(tickprefix="$", tickformat=",.0f")
fig.update_traces(hovertemplate="$%{y:,.0f}<extra></extra>")
style(fig).show()
```

## From "When do people come to Hubbard Hall?" — pooled category × weekday table

Relied on `tab` / `cat_order` from the pooled section; duplicated the facet chart.

```{python}
#| label: tab-cat-weekday-pivot
pivot = (
    tab.pivot(index="cat", columns="weekday", values="attendance")
    .reindex(index=[c for c in cat_order if c in set(tab["cat"])])
    .reindex(columns=WEEKDAY_ORDER)
    .fillna(0)
    .astype(int)
)
pivot.style.format("{:,}")
```

## From "When do people come to Hubbard Hall?" — pooled registration share by day, by period

Moved when the *schedule* moved, not the audience; the within-run over-time section answers the
underlying question. Relied on `regs`, `period()`, `PERIODS`.

```{python}
#| label: fig-timing-over-time
#| fig-cap: "Registration share by day of week, by period (all events)"
pooled = regs.dropna(subset=["weekday"]).assign(
    period=lambda d: period(d["starts_on"].dt.year)
)
rd = pooled.groupby(["weekday", "period"], observed=True).size().reset_index(name="attendance")
rd["share"] = rd.groupby("period", observed=True)["attendance"].transform(
    lambda s: s / s.sum()
)
fig = px.line(
    rd, x="weekday", y="share", color="period",
    category_orders={"weekday": WEEKDAY_ORDER, "period": PERIODS},
    color_discrete_sequence=CATEGORICAL, markers=True,
)
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Day of week", yaxis_title="Share of registrations", legend_title="Period"
)
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=".0%")
style(fig).show()
```

## From "How do giving and participation overlap?" — group counts table

Duplicated the chapter's bar chart (`counts` is still computed there).

```{python}
#| label: tab-overlap-counts
counts.style.format({"households": "{:,}"})
```

## From "How do giving and participation overlap?" — giving by distance band among donors

Largely duplicated the donor-geography chapter's table; its one new fact (median gift) was folded
into that chapter's table instead.

```{python}
#| label: tab-donor-by-band
donors = hh[hh["is_donor"]]
t = donors.groupby("distance_band", observed=True).agg(
    households=("id", "count"),
    mean_gift=("total_donations", "mean"),
    median_gift=("total_donations", "median"),
    total=("total_donations", "sum"),
)
t.style.format(
    {"households": "{:,}", "mean_gift": money, "median_gift": money, "total": money}
)
```
